title: MCP Store Contract Runtime Follow-up
findings: 发现 2 个新 distinct issue：global_ref 的 UUID schema 与 alias-based runtime/test 冲突；fab_archive_scan 被合同误归类为会写 store 的工具。
issues_found: [{"title":"global_ref schema requires store UUID while runtime and tests use store aliases as the qualified reference","severity":"medium","description":"The canonical global_ref contract says MCP recall/cite must surface <store_uuid>[:<uid>]:<local_id>, enforced by globalRefSchema. Runtime cross-store recall instead builds ids as <alias>:<stableId>, plan-context copies those alias ids into a field named global_ref for ranking, and plan-context tests assert global_ref values like team:KT-DEC-0001. This is distinct from missing global_ref fields: even if the promised field is added, current runtime/test conventions would violate the exported schema and make store-qualified citation validation inconsistent across UUID and alias forms.","location":"packages/shared/src/schemas/store-stable-id.ts:50","fix_direction":"Choose one contract and apply it end-to-end: either make global_ref strictly UUID-based by emitting buildGlobalRef(...) from cross-store recall/plan-context and keep alias as a separate display field, or rename alias-prefixed ids to a separate store_qualified_id/cite_ref schema that explicitly accepts aliases. Add runtime contract tests that parse real plan-context/recall outputs through the exported schema.","affected_components":["packages/shared/src/schemas/store-stable-id.ts","packages/server/src/services/cross-store-recall.ts","packages/server/src/services/plan-context.ts","packages/server/src/services/plan-context-scope-rank.test.ts"],"tags":["mcp-contract","global_ref","store-aliasing","schema-runtime-drift"]},{"title":"fab_archive_scan is marked as a store-writing MCP tool although the registered runtime is read-only","severity":"medium","description":"MCP_STORE_AWARE_CONTRACTS classifies fab_archive_scan as echoesWrittenStore=true and comments say WRITE tools fab_archive_scan/fab_extract_knowledge echo written_to_store. The actual archive_scan schema only returns ledger scan fields (anchor_ts, session_ids, dropped, covered_through_ts, already_proposed_keys), its annotations set readOnlyHint/idempotentHint, and the service only reads events.jsonl. This is not just a missing written_to_store field: the contract assigns a write-store promise to a tool that intentionally performs no store write, so clients may wait for or audit a write target that cannot exist.","location":"packages/shared/src/schemas/mcp-store-contracts.ts:73","fix_direction":"Remove fab_archive_scan from echoesWrittenStore/write-tool expectations, or redefine the archive flow contract so only fab_extract_knowledge/fab_review write actions echo written_to_store. Add a parity test that compares MCP_STORE_AWARE_CONTRACTS roles against each tool's annotations/output schema so readOnlyHint tools cannot be classified as write echoes.","affected_components":["packages/shared/src/schemas/mcp-store-contracts.ts","packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts","packages/server/src/tools/archive-scan.ts","packages/server/src/services/archive-scan.ts","packages/shared/test/store/mcp-store-contracts.test.ts"],"tags":["mcp-contract","written_to_store","archive-scan","schema-runtime-drift"]}]
